task: detect a Z-drift hidden by production XY4 on an idle qubit (K-level blind)
budget_basis: total device shots to detect at (5%,5%); same prep/measurement/target

PDET (classical kernel + minimal-loss kn.note: kernel is CLASSICAL (computed from the control model, 0 device shots); picks the variant that exposes Z with minimal 1/f-protection loss; spends shots only on that one witness.

DD/filter-function spectroscopy (blind s.note: CAN match PDET's capability but must SCAN the sequence family (no free kernel) -> extra screening shots. Comparable detection power; less efficient.

Fisher / measurement-optimal experiment.result: FAIL (QFI=0 under XY4; measurement optimization cannot expose a K-level null)

GST / error-generator identifiability.result: FAIL (Z-drift unidentifiable under XY4; needs control variation)

verdict.Fisher_experiment_design: FAIL (QFI=0 under XY4: no measurement/probe exposes a K=0 null)
verdict.GST_error_generator: FAIL (Z-drift unidentifiable under XY4 without control variation)
verdict.PDET_vs_DD_spectroscopy: SIBLINGS (same K-level / filter-function physics) -- NO shot-efficiency claim
verdict.honest_note: PDET does NOT beat DD/filter-function spectroscopy on shots. If the objective is minimal shots ignoring protection, BOTH pick 'free' (~23 shots, but Ã—1 protection). If the objective is detect-while-retaining-protection, BOTH need a (classical) filter-function/kernel computation to pick xy4_asym (~9310 shots, Ã—14.7 protection). They are closely related; DD-spectroscopy is essentially a sibling of PDET's K-level lever.
verdict.PDET_distinct_value: the UNIFIED restricted-(S,O) kernel framework: per-direction classification of K-level (control-fixable) vs readout-level (measurement-fixable) invisibility, the minimal control-OR-measurement augmentation, and honest finite-shot accounting -- NOT a shot-count superiority over DD-spectroscopy.
